methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: An AI data center at megasite will create 2,000 permanent positions, Pittsylvania County officials promise
signals.signal_reading: A 2,000-job AI data center in historically underserved Pittsylvania County could signal distributed opportunity, but these infrastructure projects typically concentrate technical roles in metro hubs while leaving operational jobs locally—reinforcing rather than bridging the capability gap.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA to boost education and community impact represents institutional investment in broad-based AI literacy and local capability-building, creating peer networks that could enable deep, distributed adoption beyond elite tech corridors.

signals.title: RC-RV partners with ed2go to launch innovative, on-demand workforce training programs - Roanoke College
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College's on-demand workforce training partnership signals platform-delivered AI education that enables rapid, shallow adoption across non-metro regions—broad distribution without the deep technical fluency that comes from sustained institutional development.

signals.title: Virginia Tech-backed energy tech hub opens in Newport News
signals.signal_reading: Virginia Tech extending its energy tech hub into Newport News concentrates advanced R&D capability in established institutional nodes, strengthening Hampton Roads' defense-tech cluster but following the familiar pattern of university-anchored excellence rather than grassroots distribution.

signals.title: EdgeCore secures $1.5B toward Sterling data centers construction
signals.signal_reading: A $1.5B hyperscale data center investment in Sterling deepens NoVA's infrastructure advantage and AI compute density, but physical infrastructure proximity doesn't automatically translate to local AI capability—it can attract talent away from other regions while concentrating benefits.
